_about: Plain-English explainers for top ORC offenses on the Hamilton County Justice Center roster. These are summaries for laypeople, not legal advice. Each charge is an accusation only; no person counted here has been convicted.

2903.01.plain: Aggravated murder. Purposely killing another person with prior calculation, while committing certain felonies, or against specific protected victims.
2903.01.tier_meaning: F1 — life imprisonment, life without parole, or capital. Most serious offense in Ohio.

2903.02.plain: Murder. Purposely causing the death of another person. (Aggravated murder, 2903.01, adds prior calculation or aggravating factors.)
2903.02.tier_meaning: F1 — 15 years to life in prison.

2903.04.plain: Involuntary manslaughter. Causing death while committing another felony or misdemeanor — no intent to kill, but death resulted.

2903.06.plain: Aggravated vehicular homicide. Causing death while OVI, recklessly driving, or in flagrant violation of traffic law.

2903.11.plain: Felonious assault. Knowingly causing serious physical harm, OR causing harm by means of a deadly weapon. Brandishing a firearm at someone during an assault qualifies even without firing.

2903.13.plain: Assault. Knowingly causing or attempting to cause physical harm to another, OR recklessly causing serious physical harm. Bar fights, simple punches.
2903.13.tier_meaning: M1 — up to 180 days jail, $1,000 fine. Becomes a felony against certain victims (officers, EMS, teachers).

2903.18.plain: Strangulation. Knowingly impeding another person's breathing or blood circulation by applying pressure to the throat or neck, or covering nose and mouth. Distinct from broader assault because of how dangerous and predictive of future violence it is.

2903.21.plain: Aggravated menacing. Knowingly causing another to believe the offender will cause serious physical harm to them, a family member, or property. Threats credible enough to put someone in fear.
2903.21.tier_meaning: M1 — up to 180 days. F5 if victim is an officer.

2905.01.plain: Kidnapping. By force, threat, or deception, restraining the liberty of another to hold for ransom, use as shield, facilitate a felony, terrorize, or commit a sexual offense.

2907.02.plain: Rape. Engaging in sexual conduct with another by force or threat of force, or when the other person's ability to resist is substantially impaired. No statute of limitations in Ohio when DNA evidence exists.
2907.02.tier_meaning: F1 — 3 years to life. Mandatory prison.

2907.03.plain: Sexual battery. Sexual conduct under specific coercive or relational circumstances (custody, family, professional authority, etc.) — broader than rape, narrower than consensual.
2907.03.tier_meaning: F3 — typically 1-5 years. F2 if minor.

2911.01.plain: Aggravated robbery. Robbery (theft by force/fear) committed while having a deadly weapon, dangerous ordnance, or while inflicting/attempting serious physical harm.

2911.02.plain: Robbery. Theft committed by inflicting, attempting, or threatening physical harm — or fleeing with a stolen item. Distinct from theft because of the force element.

2911.11.plain: Aggravated burglary. Trespass by force/stealth/deception into an occupied structure to commit a criminal offense, while having a deadly weapon OR inflicting/attempting harm OR another person is present.

2911.12.plain: Burglary. Trespass by force/stealth/deception into an occupied structure to commit a criminal offense. Distinct from aggravated burglary by the absence of weapon, harm, or occupant.

2911.13.plain: Breaking and entering. Trespass by force/stealth/deception into an unoccupied structure to commit a theft or felony. Distinct from burglary which requires an occupied structure.

2911.21.plain: Criminal trespass. Knowingly entering or remaining on premises without authorization, after notice to leave, or against express posting/fence.

2913.02.plain: Theft. Knowingly obtaining or exerting control over another's property without consent, by deception, threat, or intimidation, with purpose to deprive. The tier escalates with the dollar value: under $1,000 is M1; $1,000-$7,500 is F5; up to F1 for $1.5M+.
2913.02.tier_meaning: M1 default — up to 180 days. Felony at $1,000+.

2913.51.plain: Receiving stolen property. Receiving, retaining, or disposing of property of another knowing (or having reasonable cause to believe) it has been obtained through commission of a theft offense.
2913.51.tier_meaning: M1 default — up to 180 days. Tier ladders with value, same as theft.

2913.31.plain: Forgery. Forging any writing of another, or uttering a writing one knows to have been forged, with purpose to defraud.

2917.11.plain: Disorderly conduct. Recklessly causing inconvenience, annoyance, or alarm — fighting, threatening, insulting communication, unreasonable noise. The state's lowest tier of public-order offense.
2917.11.tier_meaning: MM — fine only, no jail unless persistent (then M4).

2919.25.plain: Domestic violence. Knowingly causing or attempting to cause physical harm to a family or household member, OR recklessly causing serious harm, OR by threat causing belief of imminent harm.
2919.25.tier_meaning: M1 default — escalates with priors (F5 → F3 → F1 with prior convictions).

2919.27.plain: Violating a protection order. Recklessly violating the terms of a protection order, consent agreement, or anti-stalking order issued by a court.
2919.27.tier_meaning: M1 default. Escalates to F5/F3 with priors or while committing another offense.

2921.12.plain: Tampering with evidence. Altering, destroying, or concealing a thing with purpose to impair its availability as evidence — OR making/presenting false evidence.

2921.31.plain: Obstructing official business. Without privilege, doing any act that hampers or impedes a public official in performance of lawful duties. Refusing to identify, fleeing a stop, blocking arrest of another.
2921.31.tier_meaning: M2 default — up to 90 days. F5 if it creates risk of physical harm.

2921.33.plain: Resisting arrest. Recklessly or by force resisting/interfering with a lawful arrest of self or another.
2921.33.tier_meaning: M2 default — up to 90 days. F4 with weapon, F3 with harm to officer.

2921.331.plain: Failure to comply with order/signal of police officer. Operating a motor vehicle so as to wilfully elude or flee a police officer after receiving visible/audible signal to stop. Pursuits.
2921.331.tier_meaning: M1 default — up to 180 days. F3-F4 with risk of harm.

2921.38.plain: Harassment with a bodily substance. Causing/attempting to cause an officer, employee of detention facility, or other to come into contact with blood, semen, urine, feces, or other bodily substance.
2921.38.tier_meaning: F5 default. F3 if HIV/HepB/HepC positive and discloses.

2923.12.plain: Carrying concealed weapons. Knowingly carrying or having concealed about one's person any deadly weapon or dangerous ordnance. Lawful with valid CCW permit; not so without.
2923.12.tier_meaning: M1 default — up to 180 days. F4 if dangerous ordnance / loaded firearm in CCW-prohibited place.

2923.13.plain: Having weapons while under disability. Knowingly acquiring/possessing a firearm or dangerous ordnance while under a legal disability — prior felony of violence, drug conviction, mental commitment, fugitive status.

2923.161.plain: Improperly discharging a firearm at or into a habitation or school zone. Knowingly discharging a firearm at/into an occupied structure or school safety zone.

2925.03.plain: Trafficking in drugs. Knowingly selling/offering to sell a controlled substance, OR preparing for shipment/distribution/transport when knowing it'll be sold. Distinct from possession by intent/conduct toward sale.
2925.03.tier_meaning: F5 default. Ladders by substance/weight up to F1.

2925.11.plain: Possession of drugs. Knowingly obtaining, possessing, or using a controlled substance or controlled substance analog. The most common felony charge in Hamilton County.
2925.11.tier_meaning: F5 default for most schedules. Ladders by substance/weight up to F1.

2925.14.plain: Illegal use or possession of drug paraphernalia. Knowingly using, possessing with intent to use, drug paraphernalia (e.g., pipes, scales, baggies designed/used for controlled substances).
2925.14.tier_meaning: MM — fine only, no jail unless involving a minor (then M2).

4510.16.plain: Driving on a suspended/revoked license. Operating a motor vehicle while one's driving privileges are suspended or revoked, knowing of the suspension/revocation.
4510.16.tier_meaning: M1 default — up to 180 days. Tier escalates with priors.

4511.19.plain: Operating a vehicle under the influence (OVI / DUI). Operating any vehicle under the influence of alcohol, drugs, or both; or with prohibited concentration in breath/blood/urine.
4511.19.tier_meaning: M1 default — minimum 3 days jail, up to 180 days. Ladders sharply with priors / felony OVI at 4 priors / 10 yrs.

4549.02.plain: Stopping after accident on public roads (leaving the scene). Driver involved in a collision must stop, exchange info, and render aid. Failure to do so is criminal.
4549.02.tier_meaning: M1 default — up to 180 days. F5 with injury, F3 with death.
